remember: Students will be able to recall the basic concepts of kinship, caste, and class in ancient Indian society.
Students will be able to identify and explain the different types of kinship systems in ancient India.
Students will be able to list and describe the major castes in ancient Indian society.
understand: Students will be able to understand the significance of kinship, caste, and class in shaping social relationships and power dynamics in ancient Indian society.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of the caste system on social mobility and equality in ancient India.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the kinship systems of different ancient Indian societies.
apply: Students will be able to apply their knowledge of kinship, caste, and class to analyze a given scenario or situation in ancient Indian society.
Students will be able to use their understanding of the caste system to evaluate the social and economic implications of certain practices or policies in ancient India.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the complex relationships between kinship, caste, and class in ancient Indian society.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of external influences, such as trade and conquest, on the kinship, caste, and class systems in ancient India.
Students will be able to develop and support a well-reasoned argument about the significance of kinship, caste, and class in ancient Indian society.

ncert_objectives: Explain the significance of the caste system in shaping social relationships and power dynamics in ancient Indian society.
Describe the major castes in ancient Indian society and their respective roles and responsibilities.
Analyze the impact of the caste system on social mobility and equality in ancient India.
board_exam_objectives: Kinship systems in ancient India: Types, characteristics, and significance.
Caste system in ancient India: Major castes, their roles and responsibilities, and impact on social mobility and equality.
Relationship between kinship, caste, and class in ancient Indian society: Analysis and evaluation.
